issuer: Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority (SDAIA)

controls.description_en: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently based on valid legal grounds including consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public interest, or legitimate interests.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently based on valid legal grounds including consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public interest, or legitimate interests.

controls.description_en: Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes, and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes, and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.

controls.description_en: Controllers must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance with PDPL, including staff training, access controls, and security measures.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance with PDPL, including staff training, access controls, and security measures.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to access their personal data and obtain information about processing activities including purposes retention periods and recipients.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to access their personal data and obtain information about processing activities including purposes retention periods and recipients.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to rectification of inaccurate personal data and completion of incomplete data.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to rectification of inaccurate personal data and completion of incomplete data.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to erasure (right to be forgotten) under specific circumstances including unlawful processing, purpose fulfillment, or consent withdrawal.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to erasure (right to be forgotten) under specific circumstances including unlawful processing, purpose fulfillment, or consent withdrawal.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to restrict processing under specific circumstances including accuracy disputes or legitimate interest objections.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to restrict processing under specific circumstances including accuracy disputes or legitimate interest objections.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to data portability to receive and transmit personal data in a structured commonly used and machine-readable format.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to data portability to receive and transmit personal data in a structured commonly used and machine-readable format.

controls.description_en: Data subjects have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes including automated decision-making.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Data subjects have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes including automated decision-making.

controls.description_en: Controllers and processors must implement appropriate security measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction, including encryption pseudonymization and access controls.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers and processors must implement appropriate security measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction, including encryption pseudonymization and access controls.

controls.description_en: Controllers must notify SDAIA of personal data breaches within 72 hours and affected data subjects without undue delay when the breach poses high risk to rights and freedoms.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers must notify SDAIA of personal data breaches within 72 hours and affected data subjects without undue delay when the breach poses high risk to rights and freedoms.

controls.description_en: Controllers must maintain Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) documenting processing purposes, data categories, recipients, retention periods, and security measures.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers must maintain Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) documenting processing purposes, data categories, recipients, retention periods, and security measures.

controls.description_en: Controllers and processors must designate a Data Protection Officer (DPO) when processing large-scale sensitive data, systematic monitoring, or public authority processing.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers and processors must designate a Data Protection Officer (DPO) when processing large-scale sensitive data, systematic monitoring, or public authority processing.

controls.description_en: Controllers must conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) for high-risk processing including new technologies large-scale systematic monitoring or sensitive data.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers must conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) for high-risk processing including new technologies large-scale systematic monitoring or sensitive data.

controls.description_en: Personal data may only be transferred outside Saudi Arabia if the destination has adequate protection, appropriate safeguards are in place, or with explicit consent or legal derogations.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Personal data may only be transferred outside Saudi Arabia if the destination has adequate protection, appropriate safeguards are in place, or with explicit consent or legal derogations.

controls.description_en: Controllers must ensure processors provide sufficient guarantees of compliance, use written contracts specifying processing instructions, security measures, and confidentiality obligations.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Controllers must ensure processors provide sufficient guarantees of compliance, use written contracts specifying processing instructions, security measures, and confidentiality obligations.

controls.description_en: Processing children's personal data requires verifiable parental consent and additional protections appropriate to the child's age and understanding.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Processing children's personal data requires verifiable parental consent and additional protections appropriate to the child's age and understanding.

controls.description_en: Processing sensitive personal data (health, biometric, genetic, racial, ethnic, political, religious, philosophical beliefs, trade union, sexual orientation, criminal records) requires explicit consent or specific legal grounds and enhanced safeguards.
controls.description_ar: [PDPL] Processing sensitive personal data (health, biometric, genetic, racial, ethnic, political, religious, philosophical beliefs, trade union, sexual orientation, criminal records) requires explicit consent or specific legal grounds and enhanced safeguards.
